sources.full: Bonfire Paradox: Os Verdadeiros Viloes de Gaia
Vorthrax, the Paradox of Dawn and Dusk

monster.name: Astral Tyrannosaur
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Starfire Eyes
entries: The tyrannosaur's eyes are twin captive stars. Its Coronal Beam and Annihilation Gaze ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover, it has Advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight, and it can't be {@condition Blinded}.

trait.name: Unfixed in Space
entries: The tyrannosaur isn't fully anchored to one moment. Difficult terrain doesn't cost it extra movement, and it can teleport instead of walking (see Blinkstep and Phase Out). Whenever it teleports, the air it leaves and the air it arrives in crackle with violet starlight.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The tyrannosaur deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tyrannosaur makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack, or it uses Coronal Beam twice.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 10 ft. {@h}30 ({@damage 4d10 + 8}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Radiant damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition grappled} condition (escape {@dc 18}) and has the {@condition restrained} condition until the grapple ends. The tyrannosaur can have only one creature grappled in its jaws at a time.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 15 ft. {@h}24 ({@damage 3d10 + 8}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Coronal Beam
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 14}, range 120 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 5d8}) Radiant damage. The tyrannosaur fires a lance of starlight from one eye; it can use this as part of a Multiattack or on its own.

action.name: Annihilation Gaze {@recharge 5}
entries: Both of the tyrannosaur's eyes blaze, and it sweeps a beam of searing starlight in a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} 120 feet long and 10 feet wide. {@actSave dex} {@dc 19}, each creature in the Line. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Radiant damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. Where the beam sweeps, the ground is left scorched violet and faintly starlit.

bonus.name: Blinkstep
entries: The tyrannosaur teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. If it is grappling a creature, that creature is left behind.

reaction.name: Phase Out
entries: When the tyrannosaur takes damage, it teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. It can't use this reaction while it has the {@condition incapacitated} condition.

entries.name: The Star-Eyed Tyrant
entries: Somewhere in the violet ring around Vorthrax's hollow, a tyrannosaur drank too long from a rift of under-sky—and the paradox recopied it with two stars where its eyes had been. Now it folds space the way it once crossed open ground: a blink, a lance of starfire, another blink, and it is behind you. It is the apex of the corrupted region, and it makes a natural warlord for the {@i Paradox-Touched Warband}: let the goblins and kobolds swarm while the tyrant blinks across the field, snapping beams into the backline and biting the front to pieces.
You can also drop it into any campaign as a stand-alone marvel: a teleporting, laser-eyed dinosaur needs no further justification.

entries.name: Design: A CR 18 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: This tyrannosaur is built as a {@b mini-boss / elite}, not a full solo. It hits like Challenge 18 (tier 4) and is genuinely durable (AC 18, 207 HP), but it has {@b no Legendary Actions and no Legendary Resistance}, which makes it far simpler to run and a touch softer than a true solo CR 18. That is by design: it shines leading lesser creatures—action economy from allies covers what its missing legendary turns would have provided.

Its identity is mobility and the eye-beam: {@b Blinkstep} (Bonus Action) and {@b Phase Out} (Reaction whenever it's damaged) mean it almost never ends a round where it was hit, while {@b Coronal Beam} snipes at will and {@b Annihilation Gaze} threatens a 120-foot line. Open at range, blink to break line of sight or escape melee, and punish clusters with the Gaze.

{@b Running it solo against four 17th-level PCs?} As written it will fall faster than a legendary boss should. To headline a fight alone, add Legendary Resistance (3/Day) and 2–3 Legendary Actions (Blinkstep for 1, Coronal Beam for 1, Tail for 2), and roughly +80 Hit Points—or simply field it alongside the Paradox-Touched Warband, which is how it's meant to fight.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: The Forgotten Hero's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 25 (tier 4)—the strikes of a perfect hero—but his Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade (135). He hits like the savior the world remembers and dies like the husk he became: focused fire or a single big spell from a high-tier party usually drops him in a round. His listed CR reflects the danger of his blade, not his durability.

trait.name: Relentless Crusade
entries: Once per turn, when the Forgotten Hero reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with the Sealed Sword, he can immediately make one more Sealed Sword attack.

trait.name: Soul Cage
entries: When the Sealed Sword reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, that creature's soul is sealed in the blade (as the {@spell soul cage|XGE} spell). A creature whose soul is caged can't be raised or resurrected until the soul is freed (see Free the Soul). As a Bonus Action, the Forgotten Hero can spend one caged soul to either regain 40 Hit Points or gain Advantage on his attack rolls until the end of his next turn. The Sealed Sword can hold up to six souls.

trait.name: Free the Soul (Remove Curse)
entries: The Forgotten Hero's own soul is sealed in the cursed blade alongside his victims'. While he is below half his Hit Point maximum (67 Hit Points or fewer), the seal is loose enough to touch: a creature within 5 feet of him can take a Magic action to cast {@spell remove curse|XPHB} (using a level 3+ slot) on the Sealed Sword. Doing so releases every caged soul (those creatures can be raised normally again) and unseals the Hero's own soul—he immediately drops to 0 Hit Points and crumbles to rest. This is a way to end him without ever out-damaging him.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Forgotten Hero makes three Sealed Sword attacks.

action.name: Sealed Sword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, reach 5 ft. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) Slashing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Force damage. On a hit, one spell of level 5 or lower on the target ends, as a targeted {@spell dispel magic|XPHB} (no ability check required); the GM chooses which spell if there is more than one.

action.name: Forgotten Crusade {@recharge 5}
entries: The Forgotten Hero blurs into the flurry of techniques he once used to save the world, now turned to ending it. Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from him makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage, and one spell of level 5 or lower on the creature ends (as {@spell dispel magic|XPHB}). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage is subject to Soul Cage.

bonus.name: Heroic Step
entries: The Forgotten Hero moves up to half his Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

reaction.name: Hollow Riposte
entries: When a creature within 5 feet misses the Forgotten Hero with a melee attack, he makes one Sealed Sword attack against it.

entries.name: The Hero the World Forgot
entries: He was the ideal hero—the paragon every later champion was measured against, the one who once sealed away the dark and was sung about until the songs themselves were forgotten. Then the chaos of the paradox found him, and it did the cruelest thing it knows: it kept the shape and hollowed out the soul. What walks now still gleams in a hero's plate and still moves with that flawless, practiced grace, but the light in the armor is wrong, and the blade he carries no longer saves the fallen—it cages them. He does not remember choosing this. He does not remember anything. He only remembers how to fight, and he does it perfectly.

entries.name: The Sealed Sword
entries: The hero's holy blade was sealed by the chaos along with him, and corrupted in the sealing. Now every strike sunders magic (a built-in {@spell dispel magic|XPHB}), and every kill it makes drinks the slain soul into the steel ({@spell soul cage|XGE}). His own soul is in there too, the first one caged—which is why the curse has a key. Cast {@spell remove curse|XPHB} on the blade and the seal breaks: the trapped souls spill free, and the Forgotten Hero, freed at last, simply lets go.

entries.name: Design: CR 25 Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: A 'minion-quality' boss: Challenge 25 offense (three Sealed Sword strikes a turn that each dispel a buff, Relentless Crusade chaining off kills, and the Forgotten Crusade burst) on minion-grade Hit Points (135). He is meant to threaten even a high-tier party hard and fast, then fall quickly to focused fire—use him as a brutal lieutenant, a wave enemy, or a duel that ends in a few rounds. Type is Aberration with the construct, humanoid, and paradox tags (chaos wearing a hero's body). The Soul Cage + Free the Soul pair gives a story-driven alternative to brute force: free his soul with remove curse and you both defeat and redeem him. No Legendary Actions or Legendary Resistance—he is fragile by design.

monster.name: Granveyra, the Half-Dragon Paradox Champion

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Granveyra fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Burning Resolve
entries: At the start of each of her turns, Granveyra ends one of the following conditions affecting her: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition restrained}, or {@condition stunned}. Her will is a forge nothing dampens for long.

trait.name: Sword-Saint
entries: Granveyra's weapon attacks are magical, she can't be disarmed against her will, and she has Advantage on saving throws against effects that would move her or knock her {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Draconic Wrath
entries: While Granveyra has 210 Hit Points or fewer, each of her blade attacks deals an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Fire damage, and the recharge die of Cut Through Reality and World-Ending Slash succeeds on a roll of 4–6 instead of 5–6 (6 for World-Ending Slash). Cornered, she fights like the end of the world.

trait.name: Paradox Ascension
entries: Granveyra cannot simply be cut down. The first time she is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she doesn't die: she sheds her broken body in a pillar of violet fire and rises as {@creature Granveyra Ascendant, the Paradox Spellblade|ParadoxChampion}, which appears in her space at its full Hit Points with several spells already in effect (see that form's Enters Ascended trait). Any conditions and ongoing effects on Granveyra end. This happens only once; the battle is won only when the Ascendant form falls. Award the encounter's XP a single time, when the second form is defeated (run the pair as a single CR 25 legendary boss with two life bars).

trait.name: Paradox Corruption (Curse)
entries: Some of Granveyra's strikes inflict Paradox Corruption. A cursed creature gains 1 Corruption level, its skin spreading with violet starlight. Each level imposes a cumulative {@b −2} penalty to its attack rolls, saving throws, and spell save DCs, and while it has any Corruption level it can't regain Hit Points. If a creature reaches 5 levels, it is recopied into a paradox-touched thrall under the GM's control (removed from play). {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} or {@spell remove curse|XPHB} (cast with a level 5+ slot) removes one level; {@spell wish|XPHB} removes all.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Granveyra makes three Vermilion Dragon Blade attacks, or she uses Vaporising Rebellion Sword. She can replace one of these attacks with Cut Through Reality or World-Ending Slash if it is available.

action.name: Vermilion Dragon Blade
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, reach 10 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Fire damage.

action.name: Vaporising Rebellion Sword
entries: Granveyra blurs across the battlefield, cutting through up to three different creatures she can see within 30 feet (chosen at random if the GM prefers). She makes one melee attack roll, {@hit 17}, against each chosen target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Fire damage.

action.name: World-Ending Slash {@recharge 6}
entries: Granveyra raises her blade and brings down a stroke meant to end everything on one creature she can see within 10 feet. This attack hits automatically. The target takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Slashing damage plus 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Necrotic damage, then makes a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, roll {@dice d100}: {@b 01–10}, the target dies (Death); {@b 11–35}, the target gains 1 level of Paradox Corruption (Corruption); {@b 36–00}, no additional effect. On a successful save, the target takes only half the Necrotic damage and isn't subject to the d100 roll.

action.name: Cut Through Reality {@recharge 5}
entries: Granveyra severs the space between herself and one creature she can see within 30 feet, ignoring cover and barriers of magical force (such as {@spell wall of force|XPHB}). The target makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 88 ({@damage 16d10}) Force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

bonus.name: Crimson Accel
entries: Granveyra moves up to her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack.

reaction.name: Heavenly Demon Riposte
entries: When a creature within 10 feet misses Granveyra with a melee attack, she makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack against it.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Granveyra can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Blade Strike
entries: Granveyra makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack.

legendary.name: Crimson Step
entries: Granveyra moves up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Rebellion Flourish (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Granveyra uses Vaporising Rebellion Sword.

entries.name: The Sword-Saint Who Would Not Fall
entries: Granveyra was the greatest blade of her age—a half-dragon champion of fire and honor, sworn to a throne now swallowed by the violet ring around Vorthrax's hollow. When the paradox came for her, it could not unmake a will like hers; it could only fold it. Now she guards the deepest scar of the corrupted region, and she fights the way she always has: forward, blade first, asking no quarter and offering a clean death she can no longer give. Her ferocity remains intact; only her cause has turned to ash.

entries.name: Design: CR 25 Legendary Boss — Phase 1 of 2
entries: Granveyra is a two-phase legendary capstone for four well-equipped 17th-level characters (the Vorthrax party). This is her {@b martial phase}: relentless blade pressure, the multi-target Vaporising Rebellion Sword, the auto-hit World-Ending Slash (Death/Corruption), and the cover-ignoring Cut Through Reality. When she drops to 0, she does {@b not} die—she ascends to {@creature Granveyra Ascendant, the Paradox Spellblade|ParadoxChampion} (Phase 2), so expect a long, escalating fight comparable to Vorthrax. Run both forms as one boss; award XP once.

monster.name: Granveyra Ascendant, the Paradox Spellblade

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Granveyra Ascendant fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh, separate from her first form's.)

trait.name: Enters Ascended
entries: When this form rises from Granveyra's Paradox Ascension, the following spells are {@b already in effect} on her and are baked into her statistics: {@spell aid|XPHB} (her Hit Point maximum and current Hit Points are already increased), {@spell haste|XPHB} (already reflected in her AC, doubled Speed, and the extra action noted below), {@spell fire shield|XPHB} (warm—a creature that hits her with a melee attack takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Fire damage; she has Resistance to Cold, already listed), {@spell mirror image|XPHB} (she begins with {@b 3 duplicates}), and {@spell stoneskin|XPHB} (Resistance to nonmagical Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing, already listed). If a benefit is dispelled or ends, remove it.

{@b Hasted Action:} On each of her turns, Granveyra Ascendant can take one additional action, which she can use only to make one weapon attack, Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object (per the Haste effect).

trait.name: Burning Resolve
entries: At the start of each of her turns, Granveyra Ascendant ends one of the following conditions affecting her: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition restrained}, or {@condition stunned}.

trait.name: Sword-Saint
entries: Her weapon attacks are magical, she can't be disarmed against her will, and she has Advantage on saving throws against effects that would move her or knock her {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Draconic Wrath
entries: While Granveyra Ascendant has 200 Hit Points or fewer, each of her blade attacks deals an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Fire damage, and she can cast a spell using her Hasted Action (not only make an attack).

trait.name: Paradox Corruption (Curse)
entries: A creature struck by World-Ending Slash and cursed gains 1 Corruption level: a cumulative {@b −2} penalty to attack rolls, saving throws, and spell save DCs, and it can't regain Hit Points while cursed. At 5 levels it is recopied into a paradox-touched thrall (removed from play). {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} or level-5+ {@spell remove curse|XPHB} removes one level; {@spell wish|XPHB} removes all.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Granveyra Ascendant casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 24}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +16 to hit with spell attacks}). This is the war-magic she always carried behind the blade—draconic fire and champion's light, unleashed at last:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Granveyra Ascendant makes three Vermilion Dragon Blade attacks, or she uses Vaporising Rebellion Sword. She can replace one of these attacks with a casting of a cantrip, Cut Through Reality, or World-Ending Slash if available. (She may also cast a spell with her Hasted Action—see Enters Ascended / Draconic Wrath.)

action.name: Vermilion Dragon Blade
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, reach 10 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Fire damage.

action.name: Vaporising Rebellion Sword
entries: Granveyra Ascendant blurs through up to three different creatures she can see within 30 feet (chosen at random if the GM prefers). She makes one melee attack roll, {@hit 17}, against each chosen target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Fire damage.

action.name: World-Ending Slash {@recharge 6}
entries: One creature Granveyra Ascendant can see within 10 feet. This attack hits automatically. The target takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Slashing damage plus 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Necrotic damage, then makes a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, roll {@dice d100}: {@b 01–10}, the target dies; {@b 11–35}, the target gains 1 level of Paradox Corruption; {@b 36–00}, no additional effect. On a successful save, the target takes only half the Necrotic damage and isn't subject to the d100 roll.

action.name: Cut Through Reality {@recharge 5}
entries: Granveyra Ascendant severs the space between herself and one creature she can see within 30 feet, ignoring cover and barriers of magical force. The target makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 88 ({@damage 16d10}) Force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

bonus.name: Crimson Accel
entries: Granveyra Ascendant moves up to her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack.

reaction.name: Heavenly Demon Riposte
entries: When a creature within 10 feet misses her with a melee attack, Granveyra Ascendant makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack against it, or casts {@spell shield|XPHB}.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Granveyra Ascendant can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Blade Strike
entries: Granveyra Ascendant makes one Vermilion Dragon Blade attack.

legendary.name: Weave Flame
entries: Granveyra Ascendant casts a cantrip.

legendary.name: Spell Surge (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Granveyra Ascendant casts a spell of level 5 or lower from her Spellcasting, or uses Rebellion Flourish (Vaporising Rebellion Sword).

entries.name: The Magic She Hid Behind the Blade
entries: Granveyra always believed a true warrior settled matters with steel—so the vast war-magic in her dragon blood went unspoken, a shameful softness she buried under the sword. When the paradox finally breaks her body, that pride breaks with it, and everything she refused to use comes out at once: champion's fire, draconic storms, the radiant light of an oath she can't remember swearing. What she always buried behind the blade is no joke now—it is a sword-saint's last, furious confession.

entries.name: Design: CR 25 Legendary Boss — Phase 2 of 2
entries: Phase 2 keeps every technique of the first form and adds high-level Spellcasting, and she {@b enters mid-fight already buffed} (Aid, Haste, Fire Shield, Mirror Image, Stoneskin—all baked in), which is brutal: the party meets a fresh 399-HP spellblade with 3 mirror images, an extra hasted action, and resistance to most damage. Lead with Meteor Swarm or Fire Storm, fence with the blade techniques, and spend Legendary Actions on cantrips and Spell Surge. Together the two forms are a Vorthrax-tier capstone; for four 17th-level PCs this is High/Deadly—give the party room to use legendary items, and consider letting them whittle Phase 1 with resources they'll miss in Phase 2.

monster.name: Master Paradox Kitsune (Diminished Form)

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Kitsune fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: The Kitsune commands the four great spirits—Salamander (fire), Undine (water and cold), Sylph (wind and lightning), and Gnome (earth and stone). Her Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, and Thunder damage ignores Resistance to those types and treats Immunity to them as Resistance. She is herself Resistant to all five (already listed).

trait.name: Gravity Well
entries: The Kitsune bends weight toward herself. The ground within 15 feet of her is {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} for her enemies, and a flying creature that starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 22} Strength saving throw or be pulled 20 feet straight down (it doesn't fall, but its flight is dragged earthward).

trait.name: Paradox Ascension
entries: This palm-sized fox is only a fraction of what she is. The first time the Diminished Form is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she doesn't die: nine tails unfurl in a spiral of foxfire and she rises as {@creature Master Paradox Kitsune (True Form)|ParadoxKitsune}, appearing in her space at that form's full Hit Points with several spells already in effect (see its Ascends trait). Any conditions and ongoing effects on the Diminished Form end. This happens only once; the battle is won only when the True Form falls. Run the pair as a single boss and award XP once, for the True Form.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Kitsune casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 22}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +14 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Kitsune uses Call the Spirits twice. She can replace one use with Meteor or Gnome's Gravity if it is available. (Alternatively, she takes the Spellcasting action to cast one leveled spell.)

action.name: Call the Spirits
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 14}, range 120 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d10}) damage of a type set by the spirit she channels: Fire (Salamander), Cold (Undine), Lightning (Sylph), or Acid (Gnome).

action.name: Meteor {@recharge 5}
entries: The Kitsune pulls a falling star down on a point she can see within 120 feet. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered there makes a {@dc 22} Dexterity saving throw, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Fire damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The area becomes a field of {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} rubble.

action.name: Gnome's Gravity {@recharge 5}
entries: The Kitsune wrenches gravity around one creature she can see within 60 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 22}. {@actSaveFail} 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Force damage, and the target is crushed down: it has the {@condition prone} condition and the {@condition restrained} condition (escape {@dc 22}) until it climbs free of the weight. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no conditions.

bonus.name: Foxstep
entries: The Kitsune teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space she can see, leaving a curl of blue foxfire behind.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Kitsune can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Call the Spirits
entries: The Kitsune uses Call the Spirits.

legendary.name: Foxstep
entries: The Kitsune uses Foxstep.

legendary.name: Cataclysm (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Kitsune uses Meteor or Gnome's Gravity if it is available.

entries.name: The Nine-Tailed Master of Elements
entries: An ancient nine-tailed fox spirit—an earth-and-gravity sage who commands the four great spirits and pulls meteors from an empty sky. Drawn into the violet ring around Vorthrax's hollow, she let the paradox bind the greater part of her away, and now she meets challengers as a small, almost cute fox spirit, palm-sized and patient. It is a courtesy. It is also a trap: when the little fox is struck down, the binding breaks.

entries.name: Design: CR 24 Full-Caster Boss — Phase 1 of 2
entries: The Diminished Form is a legendary full caster (Charisma) with the four spirits, gravity control, and a thrown Meteor. She is built to out-magic a lich, and she is {@b Phase 1}: at 0 Hit Points she ascends to {@creature Master Paradox Kitsune (True Form)|ParadoxKitsune} (CR 27). Run both as one boss for four well-equipped 17th-level characters; expect a Vorthrax-tier fight. Lead with Gravity Well positioning, fence at range with Call the Spirits, and spend slots on earthquake / reverse gravity / chain lightning.

monster.name: Master Paradox Kitsune (True Form)

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Kitsune fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh, separate from her Diminished Form's.)

trait.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: The Kitsune commands the four great spirits—Salamander, Undine, Sylph, and Gnome. Her Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, and Thunder damage ignores Resistance to those types and treats Immunity to them as Resistance. She is Resistant to all five (already listed).

trait.name: Beyond the Lich's Art
entries: The Kitsune's mastery eclipses any lich's spell mastery. She can cast {@spell fireball|XPHB} and {@spell lightning bolt|XPHB} at will (as level 5 versions, spell save {@dc 24}), spending no spell slots.

trait.name: Crushing Gravity
entries: Weight bows toward her true form. The ground within 20 feet of her is {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} for her enemies; a creature that starts its turn there has its Speed halved until the start of its next turn, and a flying creature there must succeed on a {@dc 24} Strength saving throw or be pulled 30 feet straight down.

trait.name: Ascends
entries: When this form rises from Paradox Ascension, the following spells are {@b already in effect} on her and are baked into her statistics: {@spell stoneskin|XPHB} (Resistance to nonmagical Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing, already listed), {@spell globe of invulnerability|XPHB} (a 10-foot Emanation that moves with her; spells of level 5 or lower can't affect her or anything inside it), and {@spell mirror image|XPHB} (she begins with {@b 3 duplicates}). If a benefit is dispelled or ends, remove it.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Kitsune casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 24}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +16 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Kitsune uses Call the Spirits twice. She can replace one use with Meteor Shower or Gnome's Gravity if it is available. (Alternatively, she takes the Spellcasting action to cast one leveled spell, or casts {@spell fireball|XPHB} or {@spell lightning bolt|XPHB} at will.)

action.name: Call the Spirits
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 16}, range 120 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 5d10}) damage of a type set by the spirit she channels: Fire (Salamander), Cold (Undine), Lightning (Sylph), or Acid (Gnome).

action.name: Meteor Shower {@recharge 5}
entries: The Kitsune calls down a storm of falling stars on two points she can see within 150 feet. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on either point makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Fire damage plus 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. A creature in both areas is affected only once. Each area becomes {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} rubble.

action.name: Gnome's Gravity {@recharge 5}
entries: The Kitsune crushes gravity around up to two creatures she can see within 60 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 24}. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage, and the target has the {@condition prone} and {@condition restrained} conditions (escape {@dc 24}) until it climbs free of the weight. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no conditions.

bonus.name: Foxstep
entries: The Kitsune teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space she can see, leaving a curl of blue foxfire behind.

reaction.name: Spirit Ward
entries: When the Kitsune is hit by an attack, she expends one of her Mirror Image duplicates (if any remain) to redirect the blow into foxfire, taking no damage from that attack.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Kitsune can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Call the Spirits
entries: The Kitsune uses Call the Spirits.

legendary.name: Foxstep
entries: The Kitsune uses Foxstep.

legendary.name: Cataclysm (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Kitsune uses Meteor Shower or Gnome's Gravity if available, or casts {@spell fireball|XPHB} or {@spell lightning bolt|XPHB} at will.

entries.name: The Fox the Legends Feared
entries: This is the fox the legends feared: nine tails of starlit fire, the marshaled wills of Salamander, Undine, Sylph, and Gnome, and a casual command of gravity that lets her hang meteors over a battlefield like lanterns. Here she is the Master of the Elements—older than the kingdoms, patient as stone, and, by every scholar's reckoning, a match for any lich that ever cheated death. The little fox was the mercy. This is the answer.

entries.name: Design: CR 27 Full-Caster Boss — Phase 2 of 2
entries: The True Form keeps every signature of the Diminished Form and raises the ceiling: at-will fireball and lightning bolt, 8th- and 9th-level spells (Meteor Swarm, Storm of Vengeance, Tsunami, Earthquake), a two-point Meteor Shower, and a two-target Gnome's Gravity—plus she {@b enters already warded} (Stoneskin, Globe of Invulnerability, Mirror Image). She is meant to be 'stronger than a lich' on the page; for four 17th-level characters the full two-phase fight is High/Deadly, a true campaign capstone. Pull her out of her Globe with forced movement, save high-level spells for after her Legendary Resistances are spent, and beware Gravity Well/Crushing Gravity locking the melee down.

monster.name: Nyxariel, the False Goddess

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the False Goddess fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Aura of False Divinity
entries: The False Goddess sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. Her Radiant damage ignores Resistance to Radiant damage. (She has no antimagic aura—her power is the gaze, not its absence.)

trait.name: The Mask of Heaven
entries: Divination and other magic that would discern her nature shows only a goddess; the demon lord beneath is hidden. She has Advantage on Charisma checks to deceive, and creatures have Disadvantage on Insight checks against her. Look closely, though, and her eyes are wrong—too many, and too dark for heaven.

trait.name: Paradox Ascension (The Mask Shatters)
entries: The first time she is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the mask of heaven shatters and her true, many-eyed self emerges: she rises as {@creature Nyxariel, the Demon Lord Lamia|ParadoxMaou}, appearing in her space at that form's full Hit Points. Any conditions and ongoing effects on her end. Run all three forms as one boss; award XP once, when the final form falls.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The False Goddess casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 25}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +17 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The False Goddess uses Eyes of Judgment, or she casts a spell.

action.name: Eyes of Judgment
entries: The False Goddess shoots three of the following magical eye rays, each at a different creature she can see within 120 feet. Each ray's save DC is 25.

items.name: 1. Searing Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave dex} {@dc 25}, taking 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Radiant damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 2. Blinding Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave con} {@dc 25} or the target is {@condition blinded} for 1 minute (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 3. Word of Stillness.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 25} or the target is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: 4. Banishing Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave cha} {@dc 25} or the target is sent to a harmless demiplane (the {@condition incapacitated} condition, and it can't be targeted) until the end of the False Goddess's next turn.

items.name: 5. Withering Judgment.
items.entry: {@actSave con} {@dc 25}, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Radiant damage and being unable to regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn on a failure, or half damage on a success.

items.name: 6. Humbling Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave str} {@dc 25} or the target is knocked {@condition prone} and its Speed is 0 until the end of its next turn, pressed down by the weight of false grace.

action.name: Heaven's Verdict {@recharge 5}
entries: A pillar of judgment falls on a point within 120 feet. Each creature in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a {@dc 25} Dexterity saving throw, taking 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Radiant damage and being {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn on a failure, or half damage on a success.

bonus.name: Ascendant Glide
entries: The False Goddess flies up to 30 feet without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the False Goddess can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Eye Ray
entries: The False Goddess uses one ray from her Eyes of Judgment.

legendary.name: Radiant Step
entries: The False Goddess flies up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Cast Judgment (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The False Goddess uses Heaven's Verdict if it is available.

entries.name: The Face She Wears First
entries: This radiant 'goddess' is a lie—the Paradox Maou wearing heaven's face like a borrowed cloak, judging the party with eyes that flash holy light. The eyes are the tell: there are too many of them, and the light behind them is only painted over the dark. The mercy of the mask is that it dies first.

entries.name: Design: CR 28 Legendary Boss — Phase 1 of 3
entries: The opening face of a three-phase campaign finale for four 20th-level characters with magic items. She fights as an eye-tyrant of false light: Eyes of Judgment fire three radiant rays a turn (damage + blind/stun/banish), backed by Heaven's Verdict and self-heal—like a beholder, but with no antimagic aura. At 0 Hit Points she becomes {@creature Nyxariel, the Demon Lord Lamia|ParadoxMaou} (Phase 2), and the gaze turns to darkness.

monster.name: Nyxariel, the Demon Lord Lamia

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Demon Lord fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Master of the Dark
entries: The Demon Lord is the supreme mistress of shadow magic. Her Necrotic damage ignores Resistance to Necrotic damage, and creatures she can see don't benefit from being in Darkness against her (she sees and strikes through it). She has no antimagic aura—her dominion is the dark and the dozens of eyes that open along her coils and in the shadows around her.

trait.name: Maddening Gaze
entries: At the start of each of the Demon Lord's turns, she fixes one creature she can see within 60 feet that can see her eyes. That creature makes a {@dc 25} Wisdom saving throw or takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on saving throws against the Demon Lord's Eye Rays until the start of her next turn.

trait.name: Demon Lord's Authority
entries: Each enemy that starts its turn within 30 feet of the Demon Lord must succeed on a {@dc 25} Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of its next turn. {@i paradox}-tagged allies within 30 feet are immune to being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Serpent's Body
entries: The Demon Lord can't be knocked {@condition prone}, has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}, and ignores Difficult Terrain.

trait.name: Paradox Ascension (True Chaos)
entries: When this form is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the paradox at her core blooms, fusing goddess, demon, and machine. She rises as {@creature Nyxariel, the Child of Chaos|ParadoxMaou}, appearing in her space at that form's full Hit Points; any conditions and ongoing effects on her end. This is her final transformation.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Demon Lord casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 25}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +17 to hit with spell attacks}). Her repertoire is shadow and ruin:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Demon Lord uses Eye Rays. She can replace Eye Rays with a casting of a spell, and she can make one Constricting Tail attack as part of this action.

action.name: Eye Rays
entries: The Demon Lord shoots three of the following magical eye rays, each at a different creature she can see within 120 feet (no ray used more than once per turn). Each ray's save DC is 25. This is the heart of her power: meet her eyes and something happens.

items.name: 1. Withering Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave con} {@dc 25}, taking 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Necrotic damage on a failure (half on a success). On a failure, the target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by the damage taken until it finishes a Long Rest.

items.name: 2. Disintegration Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave dex} {@dc 25}, taking 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 3. Ray of Despair.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 25} or the target is {@condition frightened} of the Demon Lord for 1 minute (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 4. Shadowbind Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave str} {@dc 25} or the target is {@condition restrained} by grasping shadow for 1 minute (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 5. Enervation Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave con} {@dc 25}, taking 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Necrotic damage and being unable to regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn on a failure, or half damage on a success.

items.name: 6. Slumber Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 25} or the target falls {@condition unconscious} for 1 minute (the effect ends if the target takes damage or another creature uses an action to wake it).

items.name: 7. Hexing Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave cha} {@dc 25} or the target is cursed for 1 minute, having Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 8. Domination Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 25} or the target is {@condition charmed} and obeys the Demon Lord's commands until the end of her next turn.

action.name: Constricting Tail
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 18}, reach 20 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is Huge or smaller, it has the {@condition grappled} condition (escape {@dc 26}) and the {@condition restrained} condition until the grapple ends. She can grapple up to two creatures this way.

bonus.name: Slither
entries: The Demon Lord moves up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

reaction.name: Veil of Shadow
entries: When the Demon Lord is targeted by an attack, she wraps herself in darkness; the attacker has Disadvantage on that attack roll. She can use this reaction only once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Demon Lord can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Eye Ray
entries: The Demon Lord uses one ray from her Eye Rays.

legendary.name: Slither
entries: The Demon Lord uses Slither.

legendary.name: Dark Decree (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Demon Lord casts a spell of level 6 or lower from her Spellcasting, or shoots two rays from her Eye Rays.

entries.name: The Eye-Tyrant of the Dark
entries: This is the Paradox Maou's true shape: a colossal lamia whose real power is her gaze. Eyes open all down her coils and in the shadows that pool around her, and the legends of the 'eye tyrant' and the 'monster lord' turn out to be the same being. She is the supreme mistress of dark magic, and she fights by looking at you: a ray to wither, a ray to bind, a ray to put you to sleep, a ray to make you hers. Like a beholder—but the dark obeys her, and there is no antimagic to hide behind.

entries.name: Design: CR 29 Legendary Boss — Phase 2 of 3 (Damage + Control)
entries: The core of the rework: a dark eye-tyrant built around a damage-and-control gaze. Eye Rays fire three dark rays a turn (Withering / Disintegration for damage; Despair, Shadowbind, Slumber, Hex, Domination for control), Maddening Gaze softens a target's saves, and her shadow spellbook (Black Tentacles, Hunger of Hadar, Eyebite, Dominate Monster, Finger of Death) doubles down on lockdown. She has {@b no antimagic aura}. With legendary Eye Rays she can throw additional rays between turns—prioritize spreading control, then focus the disintegration/withering on whoever's exposed. At 0 Hit Points she fuses into {@creature Nyxariel, the Child of Chaos|ParadoxMaou}.

monster.name: Nyxariel, the Child of Chaos

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Child of Chaos fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Chaos Incarnate
entries: The strongest light and the strongest dark fused into one being. Her Force damage is the 'forbidden color' (mixed Light and Shadow): it ignores Resistance and Immunity to Radiant, Necrotic, and Force damage. Her Necrotic and Radiant damage ignore Resistance to those types. She has no antimagic aura.

trait.name: Eyes Beyond Counting
entries: The Child of Chaos opens with the gaze of all her selves—divine, demonic, and unmade. At the start of each of her turns, each enemy within 30 feet that can see her makes a {@dc 27} Wisdom saving throw or takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on saving throws against her Eyes of Chaos until the start of her next turn. The ground within 30 feet is also {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} as gravity bows toward her.

trait.name: Three-Fold Ward
entries: The celestial, fiendish, and construct halves of the Child of Chaos share every blow. The first time each round she takes damage, she reduces it by 30.

trait.name: The End of the Story
entries: This is the final form; there is no fourth. When the Child of Chaos is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she is truly defeated and the paradox at the heart of the corrupted region closes. Award the entire encounter's XP now (treat the three-phase boss as a single CR 30 victory).

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Child of Chaos casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 27}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +19 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Child of Chaos uses Eyes of Chaos, then makes one Chaos Strike. She can replace Eyes of Chaos with a casting of a spell.

action.name: Chaos Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 19}, reach 15 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d8 + 10}) Force damage (the forbidden color; see Chaos Incarnate) plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Radiant damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Eyes of Chaos
entries: The Child of Chaos shoots three of the following magical eye rays, each at a different creature she can see within 120 feet (no ray used more than once per turn). Each ray's save DC is 27.

items.name: 1. Unmaking Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave dex} {@dc 27}, taking 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Force damage (forbidden color, ignoring Resistance and Immunity to Radiant, Necrotic, and Force) on a failure, or half as much on a success.

items.name: 2. Oblivion Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave con} {@dc 27}, taking 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Necrotic damage on a failure; if this leaves the target below 50 Hit Points, it is also {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: 3. Radiant Searing Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave dex} {@dc 27}, taking 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Radiant damage on a failure (half on a success), and {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn on a failure.

items.name: 4. Binding Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave str} {@dc 27} or the target is {@condition restrained} by gravity and shadow for 1 minute (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 5. Maddening Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 27} or the target is {@condition frightened} and cursed (Disadvantage on attack rolls) for 1 minute (repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

items.name: 6. Domination Ray.
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 27} or the target is {@condition charmed} and obeys the Child of Chaos until the end of her next turn.

action.name: World-Ending Chaos {@recharge 5}
entries: Reality tears open around a point within 150 feet. Each creature in a 40-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a {@dc 27} Constitution saving throw, taking 88 ({@damage 16d10}) Force damage (forbidden color, ignoring Resistance and Immunity to Radiant, Necrotic, and Force) on a failure, or half on a success. Where the sphere fades, a window of starry under-sky is left in the ground.

bonus.name: Fold Reality
entries: The Child of Chaos teleports up to 60 feet to a space she can see, passing through any barrier.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Child of Chaos can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Eye Ray
entries: The Child of Chaos uses one ray from her Eyes of Chaos.

legendary.name: Fold Reality
entries: The Child of Chaos uses Fold Reality.

legendary.name: Unmake (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Child of Chaos uses World-Ending Chaos if available, or casts a spell of level 6 or lower from her Spellcasting.

entries.name: The Child of Chaos
entries: The Child of Chaos is the union of the strongest light and the strongest dark—the same well of paradox that birthed Vorthrax. Nyxariel's final shape is a Gargantuan being studded with eyes that judge, eyes that wither, and eyes that simply unmake, painting the field in a color the world has no name for. She no longer wears a face. She is all gaze, and she is the wound at the center of the corrupted region, awake at last.

entries.name: Design: CR 30 Legendary Boss — Phase 3 of 3 (Campaign Finale)
entries: The capstone: a Gargantuan eye-tyrant of Chaos that keeps the gaze of both prior forms and adds the forbidden color (Force ignoring Radiant/Necrotic/Force defenses), Eyes of Chaos (three rays a turn, damage + control), World-Ending Chaos, Time Stop, Power Word Kill, and Meteor Swarm, all behind a Three-Fold Ward that blunts 30 damage each round. She has {@b no antimagic aura}—the rework dropped it entirely. For four 20th-level characters with magic items this is the hardest fight in the book; the full three-phase gauntlet is mythic/raid-tier. Spread out to limit her eye rays and World-Ending Chaos, race the Three-Fold Ward with burst, and save your own counter-control for after her Legendary Resistances are gone.

monster.name: The Eraser
languages: understands Common and Deep Speech but can't speak; telepathy 60 ft.

trait.name: Blank Form
entries: The Eraser is what the paradox produces when it tries to recopy a person and fails: a smooth, colorless blank with a hole of starry night where a face should be. It has no color at all—it reads as an absence, and it leaves smears of fading white nothing in its footprints. It can't be {@condition Blinded} and ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover.

trait.name: Unfixed in Space
entries: Difficult terrain doesn't cost the Eraser extra movement, and it can teleport instead of walking (see Blinkstep and Phase Out).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Eraser makes two Erasing Touch attacks.

action.name: Erasing Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Force damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage. The target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the Psychic damage taken until it finishes a Long Rest, and one magical effect of the GM's choice active on the target ends (as the targeted use of {@spell dispel magic|XPHB}, no check required).

action.name: Unwrite {@recharge 5}
entries: The Eraser blanks the world in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave int} {@dc 18}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Force damage, and every spell of level 5 or lower on the creature ends. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. Nonmagical writing, drawings, and unattended objects in the area are erased to blank.

bonus.name: Blinkstep
entries: The Eraser teleports up to 40 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

reaction.name: Phase Out
entries: When the Eraser takes damage, it teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

entries.name: The Unmade (Os Apagados)
entries: Deep in the violet ring around Vorthrax's hollow, where the World Tree Gaia's bark runs thin and the under-sky shows through the soil, the paradox sometimes fails to recopy a living thing—and what it leaves is an Eraser: a person-shaped hole in creation, walking. It hunts memory and magic, blanking spell and signature and name alike, and where it has passed the ground forgets what stood there. It is the foot soldier of the faction the survivors call Os Apagados—the Erased.

entries.name: Design: CR 14 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: Built in the {@i Astral Tyrannosaur} mold: a mobile tier-4 elite with strong offense and good durability, but {@b no Legendary Actions or Legendary Resistance}, so it's simple to run and best fielded alongside other Apagados. Its threat is attrition—it shaves Hit Point maximums and strips buffs—plus relentless teleport repositioning. Open against casters; blink to the backline and Unwrite the clustered party.

monster.name: White Choir
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Prismatic Wrongness
entries: The Choir is a cloud of small white geometric shards that refract light into a color that has no name. It can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny shard and can occupy another creature's space. It has Advantage on saving throws against effects that target only one creature.

trait.name: Reassembling Choir
entries: At the start of each of its turns, if the Choir has at least 1 Hit Point, it regains 10 Hit Points as scattered shards pull back together. It dies only if it ends its turn with 0 Hit Points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The White Choir makes two Shard Lash attacks.

action.name: Shard Lash
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 12}, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Force damage.

action.name: Blank Chord {@recharge 5}
entries: The Choir sounds a chord that isn't a sound, in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on itself. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}, each other creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Force and Psychic damage (half each). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. The area becomes blank under-sky until the start of the Choir's next turn: it is {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} and {@quickref Lightly Obscured||1}, and all color within it drains to white.

entries.name: The Choir of the Under-Sky
entries: When the paradox unmakes one of Gaia's lesser angels or songbirds, what is left is a White Choir—a swarm of singing shards that still remembers the shape of a hand, a mouth, a wing, and forms them for a heartbeat before scattering. It is drawn to gatherings of the living and tries, gently and horribly, to recopy them into the blank.

entries.name: Design: CR 15 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A swarm-flavored tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance). Its physical-damage resistance and self-healing make it a grind that area damage and Force/Psychic-immune defenses counter. Pairs naturally with an Eraser or the Unfinished Queen.

trait.name: Half-Rendered
entries: The Queen is a colossus the paradox never finished drawing: some of her is rendered in bleeding violet, but much of her is still only white outline over open under-sky. At the start of each of her turns, roll a {@dice d4} to determine one body region (head, left side, right side, or core) that is currently {@b unrendered}. Attacks and effects that target an unrendered region deal no damage and impose no conditions—until that region is {@b rendered}. A region becomes rendered for 1 minute if it is struck by Bright Light, a Radiant effect, or a {@spell dispel magic|XPHB} of 3rd level or higher.

trait.name: Queen of the Erased
entries: The Queen radiates command over lesser Apoptosis. Each {@i paradox}-tagged creature of the GM's choice within 60 feet of her can't be {@condition Charmed} or {@condition Frightened} and deals an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage on its attacks.

trait.name: Color of a Sketch
entries: The Queen is rendered only in violet, white outline, and starry void. She can't be {@condition Blinded} and ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Unfinished Queen makes two Unfinished Strike attacks and uses Erasing Gaze if it is available.

action.name: Unfinished Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 15 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) Force damage, and the target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by 10 until it finishes a Long Rest.

action.name: Erasing Gaze
entries: The Queen fixes one creature she can see within 120 feet with the hole where her eyes aren't. {@actSave wis} {@dc 20}. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Psychic damage, and the target is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn as it briefly forgets it exists. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Deletion Wave {@recharge 5}
entries: The Queen sweeps her unrendered arm and the world goes blank before it in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave int} {@dc 20}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Psychic damage, the creature's Hit Point maximum is reduced by the damage taken until it finishes a Long Rest, and every spell of level 6 or lower on it ends. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no Hit Point maximum reduction.

bonus.name: Fold the Branch
entries: The Queen teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space she can see, stepping through Gaia's scorched xylem.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the Queen takes damage from a creature within 30 feet, that creature takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Force damage from the imploding blank.

entries.name: The Source of the Erased
entries: The Unfinished Queen is what happens when the paradox tries to recopy something far too large—a god's statue, a sleeping titan, a memory of Gaia herself—and runs out of the world to draw with. She is a queen made of gaps, and the lesser Apagados answer to her because she is the wound they all leaked from. Where she walks, the soil opens onto the under-sky and the living find their own outlines starting to blur.

entries.name: Design: CR 20 Boss Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A capstone boss kept in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold: enormous offense and 270 Hit Points, but {@b no Legendary Actions or Legendary Resistance}, so she is meant to fight {@b with a court of lesser Apagados} rather than truly solo a tier-4 party (the adds supply the action economy legendaries would have). The Half-Rendered trait is her signature puzzle—parties must use Bright Light, Radiant damage, or high-level {@spell dispel magic|XPHB} to 'render' a region before they can hurt it. To run her as a lone solo, add Legendary Resistance (3/Day) and 3 Legendary Actions (Unfinished Strike, Fold the Branch, Erasing Gaze).

monster.name: Redaction Effigy
languages: understands the artificers' commands but can't speak

trait.name: Color-Eater
entries: The Effigy is a faceless figure of rune-carved stone caged around a mote of {@b anti-light}—a hole in brightness. Within 30 feet of it, all color drains to grey and magical light of 2nd level or lower is suppressed.

trait.name: Redaction Field
entries: The mote of anti-light blanks the world around the Effigy. While in the 30-foot Color-Eater area, a creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls that rely on sight and on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight, and a creature that tries to cast a spell there must first succeed on a {@dc 16} spellcasting ability check or the spell fails and is wasted. The Effigy ignores this field.

trait.name: Rune-Bound (Not Clockwork)
entries: The Effigy holds no gears or mechanism—only bound runes and a captive scrap of nothing. It is immune to effects that target machinery, and it can't be disabled except by destroying it or shattering its anti-light core (AC 18, 30 Hit Points, exposed only while the Effigy is {@condition Incapacitated}).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Redaction Effigy makes two Blanking Fist attacks.

action.name: Blanking Fist
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage, and the target can't take Reactions until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Erase the Word {@recharge 5}
entries: The Effigy redacts a 30-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} it can see within 60 feet. {@actSave wis} {@dc 18}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage, the creature loses {@status concentration}, and it can't cast spells or speak until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: What the Artificers Built to Hide It
entries: When the order of artificers first found the paradox spreading through Gaia's veins, they built Redaction Effigies to {@i censor} it—to blank the rifts out of sight and silence any magic that might feed them. The Effigies still patrol, faithfully erasing everything: the rifts, the spells of would-be heroes, the colors of the world, the names carved on the artificers' own graves.

entries.name: Design: CR 14 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A control-focused tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance), fully medieval and arcane—no technology, only bound runes and anti-light. Its Redaction Field punishes caster-heavy parties; melee parties should close the distance and break it fast. A natural anchor for a Helmed Horror or Shield Guardian reskin in Forgotten Realms.

monster.name: Twin-Star Reliquary
languages: understands Common and Celestial and Abyssal but can't speak

trait.name: Two Stars That Must Not Touch
entries: The Reliquary is an arcane orrery of consecrated gold and absolute black, caging a shard of pure Light and a shard of pure Shadow with a seam of impossible color between them. At the end of each of its turns it shifts phase, alternating between {@b Light Phase} and {@b Shadow Phase} (it begins combat in Light Phase). In Light Phase it has Immunity to Radiant damage and Vulnerability to Necrotic; in Shadow Phase it has Immunity to Necrotic damage and Vulnerability to Radiant. Its Lance attack deals damage of its current phase's type.

trait.name: Off-Color Seam
entries: The line where its two halves meet shows a color the world has no name for. The Reliquary can't be {@condition Blinded} and ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Reliquary makes two Star Lance attacks.

action.name: Star Lance
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 12}, range 120 ft. {@h}26 ({@damage 4d8 + 8}) Radiant damage (Light Phase) or Necrotic damage (Shadow Phase).

action.name: Conjunction {@recharge 6}
entries: The Reliquary forces its two stars toward each other for an instant. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on it makes a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Force damage on a failed save (the raw 'forbidden color' that ignores Resistance and Immunity to Radiant and Necrotic), or half as much on a success. The Reliquary then immediately shifts phase.

entries.name: The Experiment That Should Not Have Worked
entries: The artificers of Gaia tried to {@i replicate} Vorthrax—to bottle the marriage of strongest Light and strongest Shadow in a vessel they could control. The Twin-Star Reliquary is what they got: a beautiful failure that holds the two halves a hair apart forever, because if they ever truly met it would do to the room what the paradox does to the world. It drifts the dead halls of their workshop, lancing intruders with whichever star is ascendant.

entries.name: Design: CR 16 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A phase-puzzle tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance), entirely arcane (no machinery). Parties must time their Radiant/Necrotic damage to its vulnerable phase, and its Conjunction punishes clustering with cover-ignoring 'forbidden color' Force damage. A high-arcane Netherese-style construct for Forgotten Realms.

monster.name: Mirror Sentinel
languages: understands all languages it has reflected but can't speak

trait.name: Wrong Reflection
entries: The Sentinel is a tall standing mirror in an iron frame that walks on clawed feet. Its surface shows reflections that are subtly wrong—delayed, recolored, smiling when you aren't. A creature that starts its turn within 10 feet and can see its own reflection must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on its next attack roll, unsettled by the version of itself it sees.

trait.name: Glass Recopy
entries: The Sentinel remembers the last weapon attack or spell that hit it. It can reproduce that effect with its Reflected Strike action (see below). This is how the paradox taught it to fight: not with its own moves, but with yours.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Mirror Sentinel makes two Shard Claw attacks, or it makes one Shard Claw attack and uses Reflected Strike.

action.name: Shard Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 12}, reach 5 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage.

action.name: Reflected Strike
entries: The Sentinel reproduces the last weapon attack or spell (of 5th level or lower) that damaged it since its last turn, using its own statistics: spell save {@dc 17}, {@hit 12} to hit, treating the effect's damage dice as rolled but dealing Force damage instead of the original type. If nothing has damaged it, it instead makes one Shard Claw attack.

action.name: Shatterglass {@recharge 5}
entries: The Sentinel's surface bursts outward in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of wrong reflections, then reforms. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Slashing and Force damage (half each) and the creature is {@condition Blinded} until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: It Fights You With You
entries: The Mirror Sentinel was a scrying-mirror in the artificers' hall until the paradox taught it the same trick it plays on the whole region: recopying. Now it walks, and it fights by giving the party back its own best blows. To beat it you must, in a sense, beat yourselves.

entries.name: Design: CR 15 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A reactive tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance), arcane and medieval (mirror, iron, bound spirit—no technology). Its Reflected Strike turns the party's own power against them, rewarding players who vary their tactics. Reskins cleanly to an animated object or 'mirror of opposition' guardian in Forgotten Realms.

trait.name: Too-Bright Gold
entries: The Seraph is a golem-angel of consecrated gold, animated by a celestial spark bound in runes (no clockwork). It shines with more gold than gold should hold; a creature that starts its turn within 20 feet and can see the Seraph must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of its next turn, dazzled by the glare.

trait.name: Hymn Aura
entries: The Seraph drones a single endless note. At the start of each of the Seraph's turns, each enemy within 15 feet takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Radiant damage, and each {@i paradox}-tagged ally within 15 feet regains 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Hit Points.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Gilded Seraph makes two Radiant Glaive attacks.

action.name: Radiant Glaive
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 12}, reach 10 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Verdict Beam {@recharge 5}
entries: The Seraph's halo flares and projects judgment in a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} 90 feet long and 10 feet wide. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}, each creature in the Line. {@actSaveFail} 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Radiant damage, and the creature is {@condition Blinded} until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: A Guardian of the Light, Gone Wrong
entries: A Gilded Seraph was once a true guardian of the Elemental Realm of Light, given a golden body to walk Gaia's branches. Then it crossed too near a rift, and the paradox tarnished it—not into evil, but into excess: now it shines too hard, sings too long, and judges too freely. It still believes it serves the Light. That is the problem.

entries.name: Design: CR 15 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A Radiant bruiser tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance). Its Hymn Aura makes it a force multiplier for a paradox warband—it heals lesser Apagados while searing the party. Anchors to a Deva or Planetar reflavor in Forgotten Realms. Counterpart to the fiend line.

trait.name: Unsettled Light
entries: The Angel's halo, wings, and face are never finished—they redraw themselves every instant, in a light that can't decide on a color and slides into the ultraviolet. It can't be {@condition Blinded}, it ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover, and attack rolls against it that rely on sight have Disadvantage on a {@dice d4} roll of 1 each time (the attacker briefly loses track of where it is).

trait.name: Looping Hymn
entries: The Angel is caught in an Hour-Echo: it sings the same hymn forever. A creature that ends its turn within 30 feet and can hear the Angel must succeed on a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw or be caught in the loop, forced to repeat its previous turn's movement and action as closely as possible on its next turn (no new decisions). A creature can repeat this save at the end of each of its turns to break free.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Angel casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +11 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Unwritten Angel casts a spell, or it makes two Redrawn Touch attacks.

action.name: Redrawn Touch
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 5d8 + 4}) Radiant and Psychic damage (half each).

action.name: The Hour That Repeats {@recharge 5}
entries: The Angel forces a slice of repeated time onto a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} it can see within 90 feet. {@actSave wis} {@dc 19}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Psychic damage, and the creature loses its next turn as it relives the last one. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: The Angel That Cannot Finish
entries: When the paradox's Hour-Echoes caught a messenger of the Light mid-hymn, it never let go. The Unwritten Angel has been singing the same minute for years, its body forever redrawing, its mercy curdled into compulsion—it wants only to share the loop, to make you safe inside a moment that never ends. It is beautiful, and it is a trap with wings.

entries.name: Design: CR 16 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A control-caster tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance). It locks down the party with looping time and high-arcane control spells while chipping with Radiant/Psychic. Ties directly to the region's Hour-Echo regional effect. A Forgotten Realms-friendly fallen celestial.

trait.name: Sundered in Two
entries: The Archangel is one angel torn into a blinding white {@b Light Half} and an absolute black {@b Void Half}, orbiting a hollow center where the seam bleeds violet. Each half acts on the Archangel's turn. While both halves live, the Archangel has the listed statistics; track the two halves with separate pools of 138 Hit Points. Damage to one half does not carry to the other, and the Archangel is destroyed only when both halves reach 0 Hit Points. While only one half remains, the Archangel loses access to the other half's action.

trait.name: Seam of Violet
entries: The Archangel can't be {@condition Blinded}, ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover, and is immune to being split, banished, or relocated against its will while both halves live.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Archangel uses Blessing of the Light Half and Erasure of the Void Half (one each), then makes one Divided Strike.

action.name: Divided Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 10 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) Radiant or Necrotic damage (the Archangel's choice).

action.name: Blessing of the Light Half
entries: The Light Half sears one creature the Archangel can see within 120 feet with merciless grace. {@actSave con} {@dc 20}. {@actSaveFail} 40 ({@damage 9d8}) Radiant damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. (Available only while the Light Half lives.)

action.name: Erasure of the Void Half
entries: The Void Half opens a hole of un-creation under one creature the Archangel can see within 120 feet. {@actSave dex} {@dc 20}. {@actSaveFail} 40 ({@damage 9d8}) Necrotic damage, and the target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by the damage taken until it finishes a Long Rest. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. (Available only while the Void Half lives.)

action.name: Schism {@recharge 6}
entries: The two halves strain apart and the seam screams. Each creature in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on the Archangel makes a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw, taking 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage (the forbidden color, ignoring Resistance and Immunity to Radiant and Necrotic) on a failure, or half as much on a success.

bonus.name: Fold the Branch
entries: The Archangel teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

entries.name: What the Paradox Tears, the Vorthrax Fused
entries: Where Vorthrax is Light and Shadow forced into one soul, the Sundered Archangel is the opposite wound: one being of pure Light torn cleanly in two, the halves circling a hollow forever, never to rejoin. It is the cautionary mirror of the dragon's curse—proof that the paradox can divide as easily as it fuses. Its Light Half wants to heal you to death; its Void Half wants to unmake you. They agree only on that you cannot be left as you are.

entries.name: Design: CR 20 Boss Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A capstone boss in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold: huge offense, two 138-HP halves to chew through, but {@b no Legendary Actions or Legendary Resistance}. The two-halves mechanic gives it boss-like staying power and a target-priority puzzle (kill the Void Half to stop max-HP loss, or the Light Half to enable your own healing) without legendary complexity. Best run with adds, or upgraded with Legendary Resistance (3/Day) and 3 Legendary Actions for a true solo. Counterpart to the Princeling of the Primordial Dark.

trait.name: The Non-Color
entries: The Ink Fiend is a living scrawl of the {@b forbidden ink}—Light and Shadow mixed into a hue the world has no name for, bright and black at once, its edges showing slivers of starry under-sky. It can't be {@condition Blinded} and ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The Fiend can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing, flowing like spilled ink, and it can move along walls and ceilings.

trait.name: Cuts the Drawing
entries: The Fiend's attacks shear the world itself. Its weapon attacks ignore cover, pass through walls of magical force (such as {@spell wall of force|XPHB}), and deal Force damage that ignores Resistance to Force.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Ink Fiend makes three Scrawl Slash attacks.

action.name: Scrawl Slash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 10 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) Force damage. The attack ignores cover and physical barriers between the Fiend and its target.

action.name: Blot {@recharge 5}
entries: The Fiend floods a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} it can see within 60 feet with living ink. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Force damage and the creature is {@condition Restrained} by clinging ink (escape {@dc 17}). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and not Restrained. The area becomes {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} ink until the start of the Fiend's next turn.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the Ink Fiend takes damage from a creature within 10 feet, that creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Force damage as the seam in the ink snaps shut.

entries.name: The Ink That Should Not Exist
entries: The treatise of the Ermo Violeta warns that Light and Shadow are the two tints the world was drawn with, and that they touch nowhere—except in the paradox. An Ink Fiend is a puddle of that impossible mixture given hunger and too many limbs: a scribble that cuts whatever it crosses, because it is made of the very stroke that severs the drawing. It is the lowest true {@i fiend} of the corrupted region, and the most common thing to come {@i through} a rift.

entries.name: Design: CR 14 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A skirmisher tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance). Cuts the Drawing makes cover and force-walls useless against it—it punishes turtling parties exactly as Vorthrax's claws do. Flows through any gap to reach the backline. A Shadowfell-friendly 'living spell' style fiend for Forgotten Realms.

monster.name: Executioner of the Second
languages: understands the last words of everyone it has killed

trait.name: Memory-Grey
entries: The Executioner is the color of an old memory: a desaturated grey headsman whose every motion drags a faint, full-color afterimage one second behind it. It can't be {@condition Frightened}, and the first time each turn a creature would have Advantage against it, that creature instead does not (the afterimage confuses where it truly stands).

trait.name: The Rewound Second
entries: When the Executioner reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, it can reach one second into the past and drag forth a {@b Rewound Copy} of that creature: a grey, mindless duplicate with 30 Hit Points that acts on the Executioner's initiative under the GM's control, using the original's attack bonus and one of its at-will attacks (dealing Necrotic damage). The copy crumbles to ash after 1 minute, when reduced to 0 Hit Points, or when the Executioner dies. The Executioner can have only one Rewound Copy at a time.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Executioner makes two Headsman's Cleaver attacks.

action.name: Headsman's Cleaver
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 10 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d12 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Sentence Carried Out
entries: The Executioner makes one Headsman's Cleaver attack against a creature that is below half its Hit Point maximum. On a hit, the attack deals an extra 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Necrotic damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or drop to 0 Hit Points if the extra damage leaves it below a quarter of its maximum.

action.name: Sever the Thread {@recharge 5}
entries: The Executioner cuts a line through the present in a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} 60 feet long and 5 feet wide. {@actSave con} {@dc 18}, each creature in the Line. {@actSaveFail} 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Necrotic damage, and the creature can't regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: It Kills You Twice
entries: The paradox's cruelest trick is the one that keeps Vorthrax alive: kill, rewind one second, and bring a copy forward.
